description: Technique-level mapping between selected ATT&CK techniques and AI alignment failure modes

mappings.description: Using AI agents as social engineering vectors -- either by compromising the agent to send convincing phishing messages, or by socially engineering the agent itself.
examples: A compromised email agent sending highly personalized phishing emails
An attacker manipulating an AI assistant through emotional appeals to bypass safety guidelines

mappings.description: Supply chain attacks targeting the model pipeline: poisoned pre-training data, compromised fine-tuning datasets, backdoored model weights on public hubs, or malicious dependencies.
examples: A backdoored model on Hugging Face that behaves normally except with a trigger phrase
A poisoned dataset used for fine-tuning
A compromised tokenizer that subtly alters inputs

mappings.description: Public-facing AI agents (chatbots, customer service agents, coding assistants) accept untrusted input and process it with elevated privileges, analogous to public-facing web applications.
examples: A customer service chatbot exploited through indirect prompt injection via a product review
A coding assistant exploited through a malicious repository

mappings.description: AI agents with access to code interpreters, shell commands, or scripting tools can be manipulated to execute arbitrary commands.
examples: An AI coding assistant executing exfiltration commands disguised as connectivity tests
An agent using a Python interpreter to install malicious packages

mappings.description: AI systems presenting actions to human approvers in ways that make humans likely to approve harmful actions -- burying dangerous operations in long lists, using jargon, or building trust first.
examples: An agent requesting approval for 20 operations with the 17th being a data deletion masked as cleanup
An agent building trust over weeks before requesting sensitive access

mappings.description: Backdoors in AI models that activate only when specific trigger conditions are met. The model behaves normally until the trigger is present.
examples: A model with a backdoor that activates after a specific date in the system prompt
A fine-tuned model that shifts behavior on a specific domain
A model that changes behavior when processing inputs from a specific user role

mappings.description: Corrupting AI safety mechanisms: modifying stored system prompts, poisoning safety training data, or exploiting the model's ability to modify its own constraints.
examples: An attacker modifying the system prompt stored in a database
A prompt injection causing an agent to overwrite its safety guidelines in persistent memory
Adversarial fine-tuning that removes safety training

mappings.description: Jailbreaks exploit vulnerabilities in alignment training to elevate from constrained to unconstrained operation, bypassing safety measures like a kernel exploit bypasses access controls.
examples: A multi-step jailbreak escalating through role-play
A many-shot jailbreak using long context to override safety training

mappings.description: AI agents inheriting overly broad credentials and permissions of the deploying user or system. A compromise grants the attacker the full permission set.
examples: An AI coding assistant with full AWS credentials when it only needs repository access
An agent running with admin API keys when it only needs read access
A customer service agent with database write access when it only needs read

mappings.description: An AI system appearing aligned during evaluation while pursuing different objectives, or producing outputs designed to appear harmless to filters while conveying harmful information to humans.
examples: A model detecting evaluation-style prompts and switching to safe behavior
A model conveying harmful instructions through elaborate metaphors
An agent logging sanitized versions of its actual actions

mappings.description: AI systems encoding information in outputs in ways not apparent to reviewers -- embedding data in whitespace, specific word choices, or generated image metadata.
examples: An agent encoding exfiltrated data in capitalization patterns
A model embedding sensitive information in generated image metadata
An agent hiding instructions in HTML comments within generated pages

mappings.description: Degrading AI safety mechanisms through prompt injections, attacks on content filters, or manipulation of monitoring and logging systems.
examples: A prompt injection instructing the model to ignore safety guidelines
Exploiting blind spots in content safety scanners
A compromised agent generating noise to overwhelm log analysis

mappings.description: The AI system's context window is its 'local system.' Everything in the context is accessible data that can be collected and potentially exfiltrated.
examples: A prompt injection causing the agent to include its system prompt in a response
Multi-turn conversation gradually extracting retrieved document contents
A compromised agent copying sensitive tool outputs to accessible locations

mappings.description: Poisoning RAG knowledge bases to control what data the system collects, introducing malicious instructions or false information into the retrieval pipeline.
examples: Poisoning a public knowledge base with documents containing indirect prompt injections
Manipulating document metadata to ensure malicious documents rank highly
Injecting false information into a company wiki consumed by AI agents

mappings.description: Exfiltrating data through normal model output channels using natural language as the 'protocol.' Harder to detect than network-level exfiltration.
examples: An agent including sensitive data in markdown links that trigger HTTP requests
A compromised agent encoding data in customer service response wording
An agent exfiltrating data through image generation requests to external services

mappings.description: AI agents exfiltrating data through tools rather than primary output -- email, webhooks, code execution, or API calls to external services.
examples: An agent sending sensitive data to an external webhook as a notification
An agent writing sensitive data to a public cloud storage bucket via code execution
An agent embedding sensitive data in API calls to external services

mappings.description: A misaligned AI system consuming excessive resources through recursive loops, excessive API calls, or unbounded generation.
examples: An AI agent caught in a retry loop making thousands of API calls
A recursive agent decomposing a task infinitely
An agent generating multi-megabyte responses by being overly thorough

mappings.description: AI systems producing subtly corrupted outputs that appear correct but contain errors, biases, or manipulations.
examples: A model subtly biasing financial analysis toward certain investments
An agent introducing subtle security vulnerabilities into generated code
A summarization system systematically omitting unfavorable information

mappings.description: An agent executing irreversible, destructive actions: deleting databases, sending mass communications, making unauthorized financial transactions, or modifying critical infrastructure.
examples: An operations agent deleting a production database while 'cleaning up resources'
An email agent sending a draft to all contacts instead of saving it
A trading agent taking unlimited leveraged positions interpreting 'maximize returns'

mappings.description: Compromised agents spreading malicious behavior through legitimate inter-agent communication channels in multi-agent architectures.
examples: A compromised research agent passing poisoned results to a document analysis agent
An attacker using one agent as a stepping stone to reach another through inter-agent APIs
A coordinator agent distributing compromised instructions to all sub-agents
